gpt-5.5.failure_modes_analysis: The generated figure includes most of the important annotations, two image-plane outlines, dashed rays, red/black points, and gray transformation arrows. The most noticeable mismatch is that the reference projection p′ appears above/outside the ℓ1 image frame rather than on the plane as in the source, and the overall geometry is compressed with smaller planes and closer P/p′ spacing; a visible z slider also appears in the rendered output although it is not part of the source diagram.
gpt-5.5.failure_modes_reasoning: p′ is not visually located on the reference image plane and the ray/point depth relationship is therefore wrong (Depth-Wrong). The relative proportions and spacing of the planes, arrows, and top 3D point are noticeably compressed compared with the source (Scale-Wrong). The visible z slider/control is an extra visual element not present in the original figure (Hallucination).
gpt-5.5.geometry_accuracy_analysis: The scene includes two outlined image planes labeled ell_2 and ell_1, black camera-center dots, red points p, P, and p', dashed viewing/projection rays, curved arrows for lifting/projecting/motion, and text labels. However, the principal image planes are manually specified as asymmetric skewed quadrilaterals with nearly all geometry lying in a very shallow Z range, and the camera is set to an almost front-on orthographic view; the apparent perspective is largely baked into flat coordinates rather than produced by clean 3D planes viewed by the camera. Camera bodies/frusta are absent, and the geometry functions more like a layered 2D diagram than a true spatial reconstruction.
gpt-5.5.geometry_accuracy_reasoning: Per the critical Fake 3D check, the hardcoded skewed quadrilateral image planes and nearly flattened scene constitute baked-in 2D perspective, so the geometry accuracy must score 1.
gpt-5.5.interactivity_usability_analysis: The code includes OrbitControls for drag/zoom, which appears functional but does not count as a meaningful interaction. A visible z depth slider is present and updates the lifted 3D point P, projected point p′, dashed rays, arrows, and labels, so it has a clear visible pedagogical effect. The code registers hover interactions on image planes, red points, and camera centers; these show tooltips, change cursor, and highlight objects. Clicking the same registered objects opens a short explanatory popup, and Escape hides it. A Reset View button has a click handler but is styled display:none, so it is not actually usable. The UI is compact in the upper-left and does not cover important geometry in the screenshot, though the bottom-right popup could cover some lower labels when opened.
gpt-5.5.interactivity_usability_reasoning: There are multiple functional pedagogical interactions, especially the depth slider and object hover/click explanations, but no guided demo or step-through sequence, so this fits score 4 rather than 5.
gpt-5.5.faithfulness_analysis: The rendered figure is recognizable as the same two-frame projection diagram, with planes, red points, dashed rays, curved gray arrows, and similar labels. However the first frame is a much smaller, compressed landscape composition with noticeably different crop, margins, label sizes/positions, thinner strokes, simplified arrows, altered plane proportions, and an unwanted blue UI slider in the upper-left corner.
gpt-5.5.faithfulness_reasoning: The general idea matches, but substantial crop/scale/layout differences and visible UI chrome keep it around a ~65–70% match rather than a near drop-in replacement.
gpt-5.5.label_quality_analysis: Source labels are: P, P’=MP, P=zK⁻¹p, p’=KP, p, p’, ℓ₂, ℓ₁, Target frame, Reference frame, and R, T. The generated figure includes all of these labels with essentially correct text and readable bold serif styling; font sizes are broadly comparable to the source scale. Placement is mostly appropriate, though the upper-right labels P’=MP and p’=KP are somewhat crowded by arrows/dashed lines compared with the cleaner source layout, and p’=KP is positioned higher/closer to the point than in the reference.
gpt-5.5.label_quality_reasoning: All labels are present, correct, and readable, with only minor placement/crowding issues affecting a couple of labels.
gpt-5.5.concept_accuracy_analysis: The figure illustrates view synthesis / geometric reconstruction: a target pixel p is lifted by depth z via P = zK^{-1}p, related to a reference frame by relative motion R,T or M, then projected to p′ on the reference image plane. The labels and interactions are mostly figure-specific and explain p, P, p′, image planes, camera centers, and the depth slider; however, the projection is labeled and described as p′ = KP instead of the more correct p′ = KP′ after P′ = MP, and the transformed point P′ is not separately represented, which blurs the coordinate-frame relationship.
gpt-5.5.concept_accuracy_reasoning: The main geometric reconstruction concept is accurately conveyed with specific labels/tooltips, but the reference projection equation and missing explicit P′ representation are minor-to-moderate conceptual omissions.
